description: Using blocking (=) assignments inside a clocked always_ff block, creating simulation races and RTL/gate divergence.
body: Failure mode: a clocked process uses = instead of <=, so the simulator's evaluation order determines results and pipeline registers collapse, while gate-level behaves differently. Detection signals: lint warns about blocking-in-sequential; RTL sim and gate-level diverge; a register stage seems to disappear. Do not use blocking assignments in always_ff. Safer fallback: use non-blocking (<=) for all sequential state, keep blocking for always_comb only, and re-run gate-level to confirm equivalence.
